overall_cve.delta_vs_prior_run: April MTD moved 5037 (day 25) → 5083 (day 26): +46 CVEs net over 24h. Extrapolation moved 6044 → 5865 (-3.0%). Day-over-day rate (46 CVE/day) is well below the running April average (~196/day) and consistent with a Sunday lull. Week-17 (Apr 20-26) partial count: 1353 → 1399 (+46, fully accounts for today's pubs). Counter-argument: extrapolation will tighten as the remaining 4 days resolve and NVD backfills late publications; even at 5865, April lands meaningfully below March (6304) and consistent with the Q1 churn band (4808-6304). No positive evidence of a Glasswing-driven volume shock. Conversely, NVD publication latency of 4-8 weeks means Mythos disclosures from April may not appear in NVD until mid-May at earliest.

kev_additions.reconciliation_note: CISA pushed a new catalogVersion 2026.04.24 since prior run (was 2026.04.23). 4 new entries on 2026-04-24. None of the new entries hit the watch list. Total KEV: 1579 → 1583 (+4). Windowed (2021+) KEV: 887 → 891 (+4). All 4 new entries are 2024- or 2025-era CVEs being newly added to the catalog (lag pattern: not new disclosures, just KEV promotions of older actively-exploited CVEs).

CVE-2024-7399 — Samsung MagicINFO 9 Server (2026-04-24). Digital signage management server with HTTP-adjacent attack surface.
CVE-2025-29635 — D-Link DIR-823X (2026-04-24). Router firmware — outside HTTP-parsing scope.
CVE-2026-39987 — Marimo pre-auth RCE (2026-04-23, watch-list confirmed prior run).
CVE-2026-33825 — Microsoft Defender BlueHammer LPE (2026-04-22, watch-list confirmed prior run).
CVE-2026-20122 / 20128 / 20133 — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager cluster (2026-04-20). Cisco is a Glasswing participant.
CVE-2026-34197 — Apache ActiveMQ Jolokia RCE (2026-04-16). Claude-assisted disclosure.

glasswing_cves.note: No new Claude-credited CVEs surfaced in the last 24h. Firefox MFSA 2026-30 coverage continues to dominate but no follow-on participant disclosure detected. AISLE blog post (2026-04-22) on 5-of-7 OpenSSL April vulnerabilities being AI-uncovered remains the most recent fresh datapoint. 'Mythos Preview' Bloomberg/Fortune/Euronews coverage on the Discord-linked third-party vendor breach (2026-04-22/23) remains the dominant Mythos news story but creates no direct CVE attribution today. No new participant products (still 13 Glasswing companies).

exploit_intelligence.watch_list_status_summary: 14 watch-list CVEs total. Maturity breakdown unchanged from 2026-04-25T04:05Z full scan: 5 weaponized (CVE-2026-39987, -33825, -32201, -34197, -34621), 1 functional (CVE-2026-21858 n8n), 2 PoC (CVE-2026-40477, -40478 Thymeleaf), 6 none (wolfSSL pair + Cisco quartet). 5 of 14 (36%) have public exploit code or weaponized status. No newly-escalated entries in the last 24h.
new_observations: SimpleHelp pair (CVE-2024-57726/57728) added to KEV 2026-04-24 — pre-existing weaponization (DragonForce ransomware precursor) is now recorded by CISA. Not on our HTTP-parsing watch list, but a relevant data point on KEV-as-validator: known-exploited bugs do eventually land on KEV, just with lag (these are ~14-month-old CVEs).

Samsung MagicINFO (CVE-2024-7399) added to KEV — ~21-month KEV-promotion lag from disclosure to KEV. Reinforces the prior-run note that KEV is a lagging indicator for systematic exploitation of niche enterprise software.
exploit_intelligence.summary: 5 of 14 watch list CVEs have public exploits or weaponized status. 0 newly escalated since prior run. Last full ExploitDB/Metasploit/GitHub PoC sweep: 2026-04-25T04:05Z. Next due: 2026-04-28.

google_news_hits.headline: Mozilla fixes 271 Firefox vulnerabilities found by Anthropic's Claude Mythos in a single evaluation pass

cves: MFSA 2026-30 (41 CVE entries; 3 Claude-credited per advisory)
google_news_hits.delta: No new fresh story today; coverage continues.

google_news_hits.headline: Hackers breach Anthropic's 'too dangerous to release' Mythos AI model — Discord-linked group
google_news_hits.note: Discord group accessed Mythos Preview by guessing the URL based on Anthropic's naming conventions for unreleased models. No CVEs implicated yet but creates an exploit-pressure asymmetry — leaked-access actors can scan for bugs while defenders cannot. This is the most consequential Mythos story of the cycle and remains the dominant narrative as of 2026-04-26.

google_news_hits.headline: AISLE Uncovered 5 of 7 OpenSSL Vulnerabilities in the April 2026 Release
google_news_hits.note: Independent AI vulnerability finder claims; not Mythos but the 'AI scan tier' frame applies.

vendor_advisory_flags.product: Cortex XSOAR / XSIAM (Microsoft Teams Marketplace integration)
vendor_advisory_flags.notes: Carry-over flag from 2026-04-25 run. Palo Alto Networks is a Glasswing participant. CVE is HTTP-adjacent (Teams marketplace integration uses webhook/REST). No third-party researcher credit. Bug pattern (signature verification) matches automated-scan signal. STILL OUTSTANDING for analyst review and probable-participant table.
Already on watch-list / probable-participant tracking. Confirmed active exploitation per Cisco PSIRT. KEV-listed. Cisco is a Glasswing participant.

np_di_candidates.rationale: HTTP-adjacent management console, but Samsung is not a Glasswing participant and the CVE is from 2024 (long-tail KEV promotion). Bug type TBD. Watch for follow-on PoC publication; if NP+DI confirmed by NVD CWE, consider for watch list.

Zip-slip via admin upload — admin-authenticated only, so the NP+DI emergency-tier criteria (pre-auth) aren't met. But it's an HTTP-adjacent path-traversal pattern in widely deployed remote support tooling — relevant data point, not a watch-list candidate.

Auth bypass / privilege escalation via API — fits the widened-DI definition (auth-boundary input manipulation), but SimpleHelp is niche and not a tier-1 web stack. Note for analyst — useful as a thesis-validation example (auth-boundary widening was the right call).
Router firmware bug is outside our HTTP-parsing-software-stack scope (we explicitly exclude network device firmware in the watch-list scope rules). Logged for completeness only.

np_di_candidates.product: Palo Alto Cortex XSOAR / XSIAM Microsoft Teams Marketplace integration
np_di_candidates.np_component: Microsoft Teams marketplace HTTP callback / webhook
np_di_candidates.rationale: Carry-over from prior run — analyst hasn't acted yet. Strong fit: Glasswing participant vendor (Palo Alto), HTTP-adjacent integration, no third-party researcher credit, CWE-347 within widened-DI definition (auth bypass via input manipulation), CVSS 9.2. Probable participant self-scan.

projection_comparison.note: Both conservative and aggressive scenarios were anchored to 6044 in the prior run. Today's extrapolation (5865) lands ~3% below that anchor. Re-anchored to 5865. Counter-argument: NVD publication lag means actual April content will continue to grow over the next 4-8 weeks; the extrapolation is a lower bound. The cumulative pattern of below-conservative monthly extrapolations across April still gives no positive evidence of an AI-driven CVE-volume inflection — but this is consistent with NVD-lag eating any signal that exists.
